sections.paragraph:
συντελέω [σύν, τελέω]

—1. ‘bring to a close’

—a. of a temporal period complete, finish Lk 2:21 v.l.; 4:2; Ac 21:27.

—b. of things viewed in terms of a sequence finally coming to an end, pass. in act. sense come to an end Mk 13:4; give out in sense of there being no more J 2:3 v.l.

—2. ‘bring about in accord with purpose’, conclude Lk 4:13; bring about, effect Hb 8:8. In Ro 9:28 the ptc. συντελῶν functions adv.: effectually.
